segments.text: The word Drishi Adi Bhi is significant.
According to Jiva Goswami, Drishi means Gyana, philosophical research.
By different processes of philosophical research under different concepts,
such as the process of Gyana Yoga,
the same Bhagavan or the Supreme Personality of Godhead is understood as impersonal Brahman.
Similarly, by the eightfold yoga system, he appears as the Paramatma.
But in pure Krishna consciousness or knowledge in purity,
when one tries to understand the supreme absolute truth,
one realizes him as the supreme person.
The transcendence is realized simply on the basis of knowledge.
The words used here, Paramatma, Eshwara, Puman are all transcendental
Supersoul is also described as Purusha,
but the word Bhagavan directly refers to the Supreme Personality of Godhead
who is full of opulence, six opulence,
wealth, fame, strength, beauty, knowledge, and renunciation.
He is the personality of Godhead in different spiritual skies.
The various descriptions of Paramatma, Eshwara, and Puman
indicate that the expansions of the Supreme Personality of Godhead are unlimited.
Ultimately, to understand the Supreme Personality of Godhead,
one has to accept Bhakti Yoga.
By executing Gyana Yoga or Dhyana Yoga,
one has to eventually approach the Bhakti Yoga platform,
Paramatma, Eshwara, Puman, etc. are all clearly understood.
It is recommended in the Second Chant of Srimad Bhagavatam
that whether one is a devotee or a fruitive actor or a liberationist,
if he is intelligent enough, he should engage himself with all seriousness
in the process of devotional service.
It is also explained that whatever one desires,
which is obtainable by fruitive activities,
even if one wants to be elevated to higher planets,
can be achieved simply by execution of Dhyana Yoga,
Since the Supreme Lord is full in six opulences,
he can bestow any one of them upon the worshipper.
The one Supreme Personality of Godhead reveals himself to different thinkers
as the Supreme Person or Impersonal Brahman or Paramatma.
Impersonalists merge into the Impersonal Brahman,
but that is not achieved by worshipping the Impersonal Brahman.
If one takes to devotional service,
and at the same time desires to merge into the existence of Supreme Lord,
If someone desires at all to merge into the existence of Supreme,
he has to execute devotional service.
The devotee can see the Supreme Lord face to face,
but the Jnani, the Empiric Philosopher or Yogi cannot.
They cannot be elevated to the position of associates of the Lord.
There is no evidence in the scriptures stating that by cultivating knowledge
or by worshipping the Impersonal Brahman,
one can become a personal associate of the Supreme Personality.
Nor by executing the yogic principles
can one become an associate of the Supreme Godhead.

because the impersonal effulgence of the Brahmajyoti
covers the face of the Supreme Lord.
